ogTitle: Thompson Beach vs Thornlea: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thompson Beach and Thornlea.
heading: Thompson Beach vs Thornlea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thompson Beach, South Australia and Thornlea, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thornlea has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Thompson Beach (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thompson Beach or Thornlea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thompson Beach has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Thornlea scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thompson Beach scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Thornlea scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thompson Beach or Thornlea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thornlea has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
